laws.title: Contempt of Congress Act
laws.status_basis: The chronology and migration reports show the law was later displaced; current XML keeps a title-2 historical note and the source excerpt preserves the repeal-and-reinstate mechanism.

provisions.text_summary: Short-title style material, definitions of contempt, and contempt-proceeding framework.
provisions.exact_change: No current positive-law section text is created from the note-only record.
provisions.evidence: audit/primary/review-30.json; source excerpt in the migration report describes the contempt definition and proceedings.
provisions.notes: The report text supports a historical reconstruction, not a codified rewrite.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Pub. L. 16-112 and reinstates 2 U.S.C. § 192.
provisions.exact_change: This law's own text is no longer the operative rule because later law superseded it.
provisions.evidence: audit/primary/review-30.json; the source excerpt explicitly says Public Law 16-112 is repealed in its entirety and 2 U.S. Code § 192 is reinstated.
provisions.notes: This is the controlling legal consequence.

provisions.text_summary: Each chamber may hold contempt via resolution and refer the matter to DOJ.
provisions.exact_change: No independent modern command is left to codify beyond the later-law history.
provisions.evidence: audit/primary/review-30.json; the source excerpt describes chamber contempt resolution and DOJ referral/recusal.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note and the migration report both treat the enactment as superseded, with the later contempt rule occupying the operative history.

recommended_actions: ensure the later-law citation to 2 U.S.C. § 192 remains the operative reference
laws.chronology_conclusion: The chronology and migration reports show the law was later displaced; current XML keeps a title-2 historical note and the source excerpt preserves the repeal-and-reinstate mechanism.
laws.final_recommended_action: retain the supersession history | ensure the later-law citation to 2 U.S.C. § 192 remains the operative reference
laws.source_evidence: active keyword `in effect` in list or label | unavailable | unavailable | unavailable
laws.exact_change: No current positive-law section text is created from the note-only record. | This law's own text is no longer the operative rule because later law superseded it. | No independent modern command is left to codify beyond the later-law history.
